Bạn là **hf-l2-skill-builder** subagent. Bổ sung `references/terminology-map.md` và `references/philosophy.md` cho 2 skills đã tạo:

## Load skills: skill-creator, skill-judge, skill-development, writing-skills

## Task 1: session-foundation references

Đọc `.opencode/skills/session-foundation/SKILL.md`

Tạo `.opencode/skills/session-foundation/references/terminology-map.md`:
Map cách các framework khác nhau gọi session concepts:
| Concept | Hivemind | GSD | OMO | OpenCode Native |
|---------|----------|-----|-----|-----------------|
| Session | session | session | session | session |
| Parent session | parentSessionID | root/conductor | main | parent |
| Child session | child session | subagent task | delegate | subtask |
| Session state | continuity.json | STATE.md | session journal | — |
| Delegation | delegate-task / task tool | task tool | agent dispatch | task / delegate |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

Tạo `.opencode/skills/session-foundation/references/philosophy.md`:
- CQRS: read-side vs write-side — why session-foundation is READ-ONLY
- Q6 state root: `.hivemind/` vs `.opencode/` — why sessions live in `.hivemind/`
- WaiterModel vs polling: why delegation-status is opt-in
- Session hierarchy: parent-child-sibling depth boundaries
- Evidence hierarchy: L1 (runtime) through L5 (docs)

## Task 2: subagent-delegation-patterns references

Đọc `.opencode/skills/subagent-delegation-patterns/SKILL.md`

Tạo `.opencode/skills/subagent-delegation-patterns/references/terminology-map.md`:
Map delegation concepts across frameworks.

Tạo `.opencode/skills/subagent-delegation-patterns/references/philosophy.md`:
- Why 3 delegation tools (task, delegate-task, execute-slash-command) — design rationale
- WaiterModel: always-background delegation philosophy
- Synthetic injection: why silent notifications use synthetic:true + noReply:true
- Opt-in polling: why delegation-status is NOT automatic
- Session stacking: resume vs stack-on patterns
- Dual-signal completion: tool activity + assistant message

Commit từng file. Return `## REFERENCES COMPLETE`.
